title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the game-winning goal 39 seconds into overtime, securing a 2-1 victory for the Bruins over the Kings.
Despite dental injuries from a previous slap shot, McAvoy returned to play after being hit during the game.
McAvoy's resilience and performance could position him as a leading figure in the Bruins' leadership core.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected after receiving two technical fouls in the game against the San Antonio Spurs.
Brown criticized the referees on social media following his ejection, expressing frustration with the officiating.
The Celtics were shorthanded with Payton Pritchard and Nikola Vucevic out, losing 125-116 to the Spurs.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, was sentenced to life in prison with parole eligibility after 17 years for murdering Margarita Morehead.
Alvarez, from North Dartmouth, pleaded guilty to murder and other charges, initially pleading not guilty in September 2023.
Despite a restraining order, Alvarez remained in contact with Morehead and stabbed her during an argument on May 8, 2023.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: MBTA Transit Police seek public help to identify suspect in Feb. 27 assault at Prudential station.
Suspect allegedly spat on and hit a Green Line rider multiple times with a closed fist.
Police describe suspect as a man with short hair, a beard, and tattoos on neck and hands.

title: Patriots reportedly remain interested in A.J. Brown trade after signing Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign Romeo Doubs to a four-year contract worth up to $80 million to bolster receiving corps.
New England maintains interest in trading for Eagles' A.J. Brown despite acquiring Romeo Doubs.
Eagles reportedly demand 2027 first-round and 2026 second-round picks for A.J. Brown in trade talks.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sydney Weisman, 24, from Fort Lauderdale, Florida, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients.
Weisman is part of Team Ellie, raising awareness and funds for the Ellie Fund, which aids individuals affected by breast cancer.
The Ellie Fund provides vital services like transportation, meals, and childcare to ease burdens for breast cancer patients and their families.

title: 3 Stop & Shop locations in Boston, Reading receive bomb threats, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Bomb threats were reported at Stop & Shop locations in Jamaica Plain, Allston, and Reading on Monday afternoon.
Police and bomb squads found no credible threats after searches at all three locations; investigations are ongoing.
Reading police evacuated the Walkers Brook Drive store as a precaution, but later cleared the scene.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots WR Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots signed wide receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year, $68 million contract, potentially reaching $80 million.
Doubs recorded 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns with the Green Bay Packers in the 2025 season.
In playoff games, Doubs has 20 catches for 371 yards and two touchdowns, including two 100-yard performances.

title: Southbridge woman dies after being rescued from house fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A Southbridge woman in her 60s died after being rescued from a house fire at 417 South St.
Fire officials found no working smoke alarms in the home, marking the eighth Massachusetts death this year due to this issue.
Investigators identified several accidental fire causes in the kitchen, highlighting the importance of functional smoke alarms.

title: New ‘call a boomer’ phone line connects young people in Boston to seniors in Nevada
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matter Neuroscience launched a 'call a boomer' phone line connecting Boston youth with Reno seniors to bridge generational gaps.
Two phones, located in Brookline and Sierra Manor, allow free calls and voicemail, facilitating intergenerational communication.
The project follows a successful political divide effort with phones in San Francisco and Abilene, fostering cross-generational conversations.
